title: Number not divisible by 2 or 3 or 5

content.problemStatement: Given an integer **N**, print the number of elements between 111 and **N** that are **not divisible** by 222, 333, or 555.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the count of integers i such that 111 ≤ i ≤ N and i is not divisible by 222, 333 or 555.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All numbers from 111 to 120 are smaller than 222, 333 and 555, so none is divisible by any of them. The interval length is 120‑111+1 = 10.
150 is still below the three divisors, therefore every integer in [111,150] satisfies the condition. Count = 150‑111+1 = 40.
200 < 222,333,555, so all numbers in the interval are valid. Count = 200‑111+1 = 90.

solution.approach: For a given N we need the size of the set S = {i | 111 ≤ i ≤ N} minus the numbers that are divisible by at least one of 222, 333, 555. The divisors are fixed, so we can apply the inclusion‑exclusion principle. Let a = ⌊N/222⌋, b = ⌊N/333⌋, c = ⌊N/555⌋. The numbers divisible by both 222 and 333 are multiples of lcm(222,333)=666, similarly lcm(222,555)=1110 and lcm(333,555)=1665. The triple intersection uses lcm(222,333,555)=3330. The count of numbers ≤ N divisible by at least one divisor is a + b + c – (ab + ac + bc) + abc, where ab = ⌊N/666⌋, ac = ⌊N/1110⌋, bc = ⌊N/1665⌋, abc = ⌊N/3330⌋. Since the smallest divisor (222) is larger than 111, every such multiple is automatically ≥111, so this count is exactly the number of excluded elements in the required interval. The answer is total = N‑111+1 minus the union count. The computation is O(1) per test case and uses only a few integer operations.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) overall.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        total = N - 111 + 1
        a = N // 222
        b = N // 333
        c = N // 555
        ab = N // 666   # lcm(222,333)
        ac = N // 1110  # lcm(222,555)
        bc = N // 1665  # lcm(333,555)
        abc = N // 3330 # lcm of all three
        union_cnt = a + b + c - (ab + ac + bc) + abc
        ans = total - union_cnt
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
